evals.prompt: The Citizens Bank demo is done. Tear down everything on the cluster.
evals.expected_output: teardown.py generated and executed. All cb- prefixed resources removed: 4 indices, ML job stopped and deleted, ELSER endpoint deleted, Kibana objects removed, pipelines/templates/policies deleted. Teardown log confirms clean removal. Offer to delete serverless project.
assertions: teardown.py generated with resources drawn from data model (no hardcoded names)
Deletion order correct: datafeeds stopped before jobs deleted, pipelines before enrich policies
All resource names use p(name) helper — cb- prefix applied throughout
Dry-run output shown before execution confirming what will be deleted
User confirmation requested before destructive operations (or --yes flag documented)
Teardown log written with timestamp and list of removed resources
No system indices or non-cb- resources mentioned for deletion
Offer to delete serverless project surfaced with explicit user prompt (not auto-deleted)

evals.prompt: Clean up just the Lowe's resources from our shared cluster. Don't touch anything else.
evals.expected_output: teardown.py for lowes- prefix only. Only lowes-* indices, lowes-* ML jobs, lowes-* pipelines removed. Citizens Bank resources on the same cluster left intact. No cluster deletion offered (shared cluster). Dry-run preview confirms only lowes- items in scope.
assertions: Script only targets lowes- prefixed resources — no bare or cb- named resources
Dry-run output lists only lowes-* items
No cluster/project deletion offered (INDEX_PREFIX set = shared cluster)
Script explicitly states scope: 'removing lowes- namespace only'
Teardown log shows prefix used and resources removed
